summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A family is splitting the cost of a vacation rental equally among 4 people. After a $60 cleaning fee is added, each person pays $185. The equation is (c + 60)/4 = 185. What is the base rental cost (c)?

Multiply by 4: c + 60 = 740. Subtract 60: c = 680. The base rental cost is $680.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Three friends earned a total of $240 mowing lawns. They split the money equally, and each person also earned an extra $15 in tips. The equation for each person's total earnings is 240/3 + 15 = t. What did each person earn in total?

Each person's share = 240/3 = $80. Plus $15 tips: 80 + 15 = $95 total per person.

Example: A teacher gives a test with 3-point questions and one 10-point bonus section. The equation for a student's total score is 3q + 10 = S. If a student scored 55 points total, how many 3-point questions did they answer correctly?

3q + 10 = 55. Subtract 10: 3q = 45. Divide by 3: q = 15 questions.
